description: Long fragment of a letter by a man who, among other things, discusses the learning of his son and expresses outrage that, despite an impeccable record after 60 years of business, a lawsuit was being brought against him. Gil identifies the writer as Ismail b. Ishaq al-Andalusi and dates the letter to 1050. (Information from Mediterranean Society, III, pp. 237, 477; V, 205, 565, and Gil)
